generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance BuildingControlSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic Building Control System sensor configured within BIM environment by Building Control Engineers, supporting real-time data collection for Digital Twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BcsSensorTelemetryData
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing real-time environmental metrics (e.g., temperature, humidity) for operational awareness and predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTelemetryData
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor tracking occupancy metrics to enable space utilization optimization and energy management."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyTelemetryData
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring equipment operational status (e.g., HVAC, lighting) for predictive maintenance and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusTelemetryData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance BcsSensorTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM data and operational parameters extracted from Building Control System components, transmitted in real-time via standardized protocols."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingControlSystemSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "High-frequency environmental metrics (e.g., temperature, humidity) synchronized bidirectionally between physical sensors and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance OccupancyTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data enabling dynamic space utilization analysis and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Equipment operational status data for predictive maintenance and control logic validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance EnrichedBimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model incorporating Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) as structured hierarchical representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoIngestion, EclipseDittoSynchronization
]

instance DeviceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured device models extracted from enriched BIM, defining addressing mechanisms (e.g., tenant IDs, endpoint addresses) for actuation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoSynchronization
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic representation of control logic defined in BIM models, enabling validation and refinement by Building Control Engineers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineVisualization
]

instance OperationalWorkflowModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Automated workflow models generated from MDE transformations, supporting real-time monitoring and decision-making processes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoSynchronization
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous sensor readings and actuator states synchronized bidirectionally between physical building and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BcsSensorTelemetryData, EnvironmentalTelemetryData, OccupancyTelemetryData, EquipmentStatusTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoSynchronization, TimeSeriesDatabaseStorage
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived operational data for trend analysis, predictive analytics, and lifecycle governance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BcsSensorTelemetryData, EnvironmentalTelemetryData, OccupancyTelemetryData, EquipmentStatusTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimeSeriesDatabaseStorage
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoIngestion : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight M2M service for secure, protocol-agnostic IoT data ingestion from heterogeneous sensors and edge devices."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataAggregationService
]

instance EclipseDittoSynchronization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source digital twin framework providing event-driven data synchronization and state management for cyber-physical assets."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlLogicValidationService
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D/4D rendering engine for immersive visualization, supporting VR/AR integration and dynamic scenario simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationAndCollaborationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-velocity time-series database for sensor data storage and historical trend analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and visualizes runtime data (e.g., sensor telemetry, occupancy metrics) for continuous operational awareness."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Leverages data-driven simulations to forecast system degradation, maintenance needs, or energy consumption trends."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight, EnergyConsumptionForecastInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Applies algorithmic optimization (e.g., energy efficiency, space utilization) to propose actionable improvements."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyOptimizationAction, SpaceUtilizationOptimizationAction
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers enhanced decision-making capabilities by integrating simulation outcomes, predictive insights, and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnhancedDecisionMakingInsight
]

instance VisualizationAndCollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides immersive, multi-dimensional representations (e.g., 3D BIM models with real-time overlays) for stakeholder collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight, StakeholderCollaborationInsight
]

instance ControlLogicValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates and refines control logic defined in BIM models using semantic representations of Building Control System components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures continuous synchronization between physical building and Digital Twin for timely insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationValidationInsight
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingControlSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuators (e.g., HVAC dampers, valve actuators) modeled in BIM and operated remotely by Facility Managers via Digital Twin."
]

instance RemoteControlMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Control mechanisms enabling real-time operation of physical infrastructure through Digital Twin interfaces."
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Facility Managers visualize and analyze system states (e.g., sensor data, actuator status) via interactive visualization tools."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identifies operational anomalies through real-time data comparison between physical and digital representations."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasts equipment degradation and maintenance needs to reduce unplanned downtime."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicts energy consumption trends to optimize resource allocation."
]

instance ControlLogicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validates control logic defined in BIM models against real-time operational data."
]

instance EnhancedDecisionMakingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Integrates simulation outcomes, predictive insights, and real-time data for stakeholder decision support."
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides 3D/4D representations with real-time overlays for intuitive analysis."
]

instance StakeholderCollaborationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Facilitates cross-disciplinary collaboration through shared Digital Twin interfaces."
]

instance SynchronizationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Ensures consistency between physical and digital representations during real-time synchronization."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proposes algorithmic improvements for energy consumption reduction."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SpaceUtilizationOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimizes space allocation based on occupancy and operational data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Twinning Process and DT Evolution
instance ModelDrivenEngineeringTransformation : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Automated MDE process transforming enriched BIM models into structured, deployable Digital Twin artifacts."
]

instance SemanticEnrichmentProcess : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Semantic extraction of Building Control System components from BIM models for Digital Twin integration."
]

instance AutomatedRuntimeConfiguration : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Automated generation of runtime configurations from MDE transformations."
]

instance StandardizedExtensionPoint : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Modular architecture supporting integration of custom services and plugins without disrupting core functionality."
]

instance AutomatedValidationProcess : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Ensures consistency between physical and digital representations during artifact generation."
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationMechanism : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data exchange validated through interactive visualization and monitoring interfaces."
]

instance LifecycleTraceabilitySystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Maintains traceability from BIM models to deployed Digital Twin artifacts for versioning and change management."
]

instance DynamicAdaptationFramework : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Enables iterative development and integration of custom services via standardized interfaces."
]

// C5: Multiplicities
instance FacilityManagerDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeMonitoringService, RemoteControlMechanism
]

instance ExtendedDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService, VisualizationAndCollaborationService
]

instance EnrichedBimDerivedDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ControlLogicValidationService, DeviceModel, ControlLogicModel
]

instance DigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains EclipseHonoIngestion, EclipseDittoSynchronization, TimeSeriesDatabaseStorage
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** in this DT system involves **command-driven control flows** from the digital twin to physical building systems, governed by the following technical specifications:
  - **Command Types**: Operational directives (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control, equipment activation) derived from real-time monitoring and analytics.
  - **Control Message Formats**: Standardized formats ensuring interoperability with physical systems, though exact syntax (e.g., JSON, XML, proprietary) is not specified.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Implicitly required to ensure secure and authorized command execution (referenced in **NF_X** for interoperability and maintainability).
  - **Feedback Systems**: Bidirectional confirmation loops where physical systems acknowledge command receipt/execution, enabling closed-loop control (e.g., status updates, error signals).
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **runtime configuration management** capability suggests dynamic adjustments to resolve conflicts without full redeployment.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Real-time validation of command outcomes, leveraging the DT’s **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring by *Facility Managers* (as per Figure 1)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling **live synchronization** of operational states. The framework supports **real-time monitoring and control** through bidirectional data flows, though specific numerical latency requirements (e.g., millisecond-level or sub-100ms thresholds) are not defined in the provided documents. The temporal granularity is implied to be **high-resolution**, aligning with the dynamic operational needs of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain, as opposed to static BIM representations. The system leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as the underlying integration layer for real-time data exchange, ensuring low-latency communication between physical assets and their digital twins. No explicit time scales for service-specific synchronization (e.g., control loops vs. analytics) are specified, but the framework’s design prioritizes **temporal fidelity** for operational decision-making."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based architecture pattern** centered on the **DT Framework**, which orchestrates interactions between physical buildings, BIM models, and operational services. Key components include:
  - **Enriched BIM Models** (static input) transformed into **operational DTs** via a **life-cycle-aware deployment pipeline**,
  - **Bidirectional Data Exchange** between physical assets and digital counterparts, facilitated by the framework’s **central hub**,
  - **Modular Service Integration** via standardized extension points, enabling dynamic addition of analytics or third-party tools,
  - **Runtime Configuration Management** for adaptive resource allocation and system-wide adjustments without redeployment.
  The constellation leverages **interoperability standards** (implied by NF_X requirements) to ensure seamless coordination across heterogeneous systems, while **scalability mechanisms** (from components to entire infrastructure) manage resource constraints. The system-wide coordination is further reinforced by **low-overhead maintainability** and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with Building Control System (BCS) components to create a unified semantic foundation.
  2. **Model Transformation and Artifact Generation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., runtime configurations, service definitions) with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to synchronize the physical building and its digital twin, enabling real-time operational capabilities.
  4. **Runtime Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to monitor system states, visualize data, and control physical infrastructure via immersive interfaces (e.g., interactive visualization tools).
  5. **Custom Service Integration and Evolution**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** through standardized extension points, allowing the DT to adapt to evolving operational requirements. The framework supports **modular architecture** with predefined interfaces for seamless integration.
  Transition management between phases is governed by **automated workflows** tied to MDE transformations, ensuring consistency and traceability. Lifecycle governance is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **stakeholder-specific use cases** (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for operations, DT Engineers for extensions), as outlined in Table I and Table II of the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** by leveraging **BIM’s semantically rich, object-based 3D models** to create a geometrically and semantically accurate representation of the physical building. The framework ensures **consistent runtime artifacts** through automated generation processes, minimizing manual intervention. Validity is quantified using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which measures how well the resulting DT reflects its physical counterpart. While specific validation methods (e.g., runtime synchronization checks) are not detailed, the system’s reliance on **real-time data exchange** and **automated artifact generation** inherently supports **quality assurance** by maintaining alignment between the digital and physical domains."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system’s **technical network infrastructure** is designed for **real-time, low-latency communication** between distributed components, leveraging a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) are not detailed, the framework supports **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** (e.g., BIM tools, IoT sensors, legacy building control systems). **Network topology** is implied to be **hybrid**, accommodating edge deployments (e.g., local sensor gateways) and centralized cloud/on-premise hubs. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are critical for real-time synchronization, though exact thresholds are unspecified. **Reliability and redundancy** are addressed via the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, enabling dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. **Security protocols** are **not explicitly mentioned** in the provided documents; the non-functional requirements (NF_X) focus on **interoperability, scalability, maintainability, and accessibility**, with no reference to encryption or access control mechanisms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure supports **flexible, scalable, and adaptable** deployment models, with the following technical specifications:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **cloud, edge, and on-premise** deployment (inferred from the framework’s modularity and NF_X scalability requirements).
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Designed to handle **scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, with low-overhead updates (per NF_X maintainability). Exact resource allocations (e.g., CPU, memory) are unspecified.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Leverages the **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** to dynamically integrate new services or scale components without full redeployment. Runtime configuration management enables on-the-fly adjustments.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: Includes **BIM-driven deployment** (via *Building Control Engineers*) and **operational DT deployment** (for *Facility Managers*), with a lifecycle managed by the DT Framework.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Ensured through **runtime adaptability** and **low-overhead updates**, minimizing downtime. Redundancy and failover mechanisms are implied but not explicitly detailed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration with external tools and platforms. Key integration aspects include:
   - **Data exchange protocols**: Leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as the underlying layer for **real-time, bidirectional data synchronization** between physical assets and digital twins.
   - **Dependencies**: Explicit reliance on **BIM tools** (e.g., for initial device modeling and deployment) and **operational systems** (e.g., for real-time monitoring inputs).
   - **Integration patterns**: Supports **modular service integration**, allowing third-party analytics or control services to extend the DT’s capabilities via the framework’s extension points.
   - **Interoperability standards**: Aligned with non-functional requirements (**NF_X**) to ensure compatibility with **diverse AECO domain systems**, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, IFC) or enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS) are not explicitly mentioned. The framework’s design emphasizes **low-overhead updates** and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise, implying broad horizontal scalability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **AECO-specific systems and standards** (e.g., BIM) as its foundational interoperability standard, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. **Device modeling** and **control logic definition** are standardized within the BIM environment, ensuring semantic consistency for subsequent transformations. The **modular architecture** enforces **standardized extension points** for integrating custom services, promoting interoperability with third-party tools and analytics. While the document does not explicitly cite **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** or **COBie**, the reliance on BIM implies compliance with **ISO 19650** (BIM standards) and **buildingSMART** frameworks. Compliance is further ensured through **automated model transformations** that map enriched BIM semantics to deployable DT artifacts, adhering to **model-driven engineering (MDE) principles** for governance and validation. No explicit certification requirements or additional industry standards (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect) are mentioned."
]
